data.title: Wenzhou Kangning Wins CSRC Nod for Full Share Circulation Plan
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Wenzhou Kangning Hospital Co ( (HK:2120) ) has issued an announcement.

Wenzhou Kangning Hospital Co., Ltd., a Hong Kong-listed healthcare operator based in Zhejiang, China, derives its funding from a mix of domestic shares and H shares, enabling participation by both mainland and overseas investors. The company is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent directors in line with cross-border listing requirements.

The company announced that China’s securities regulator has accepted and completed its filing application for the full circulation, conversion and listing of its shares, paving the way for more domestic shares to be converted into H shares. Following the planned conversion, the proportion of domestic shares will fall from about 72.91% to 60.08%, while H shares will rise to 39.92%, though the implementation plan and timetable remain unfinalised and subject to further regulatory procedures, prompting a cautionary note to shareholders and potential investors.

More about Wenzhou Kangning Hospital Co

Wenzhou Kangning Hospital Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed healthcare provider based in Zhejiang, China, operating hospitals with a focus on medical services in the domestic market. The company’s share capital is split between domestic shares and H shares, giving it access to both mainland and international investors through the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

Its governance structure includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard corporate oversight for a cross-border listed healthcare group. The company’s capital structure and listing status are subject to regulation by both Hong Kong and mainland Chinese securities authorities, including the CSRC.

Current Market Cap: HK$547.7M

data.title: Renaissance Asia Silk Road Group Flags Further Audit Delays, Trading Suspension Persists
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.

The latest update is out from Renaissance Asia Silk Road Group Limited ( (HK:0274) ).

Renaissance Asia Silk Road Group Limited, a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 274, continues to face prolonged trading suspension and compliance challenges with local listing rules. Its board is comprised of an executive director, non-executive directors and independent non-executive directors, in line with governance standards for Hong Kong-listed firms.

The company has announced it will delay publication of its audited annual results for the year ended 31 March 2026 and expects a knock-on delay in sending its annual report to shareholders. These delays mean continued non-compliance with Hong Kong listing rules and the ongoing suspension of share trading, heightening uncertainty for investors and signalling persistent difficulties in completing audits for two consecutive financial years.

More about Renaissance Asia Silk Road Group Limited

Renaissance Asia Silk Road Group Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 274, operates as a public company on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. Its governance structure includes an executive director, several non-executive directors and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical listed-company board composition.

The company’s securities have been suspended from trading on the Stock Exchange since July 2025, indicating ongoing regulatory and operational challenges. Shareholders and potential investors have been advised to exercise caution when dealing in its securities, underscoring elevated risk and uncertainty around the issuer.

Huajin International Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2738) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Huajin International Holdings has disclosed that equity interests in certain lower-tier mainland China subsidiaries have been judicially frozen in 52 instances, following asset preservation applications by external third-party claimants. The freezes stem from outstanding payment obligations owed by these PRC units to various creditors.

The company stressed that the measures apply only to the equity of its PRC subsidiaries and do not affect its listed shares in Hong Kong, which remain unfrozen according to official records. Management is continuing cross-checks across government and judicial portals to verify all freezing orders and identify any additional records, aiming to keep shareholders and potential investors informed of the evolving situation.

More about Huajin International Holdings Ltd.

Huajin International Holdings Ltd. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a network of subsidiaries in mainland China. The group’s PRC entities are involved in commercial activities that generate payment obligations to various external creditors, exposing them to civil and commercial disputes in the Chinese courts.

data.title: United Laboratories Wins China Approval for New Tadalafil Men’s Health Drug
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The United Laboratories International Holdings ( (HK:3933) ) has shared an announcement.

The United Laboratories International Holdings has received Chinese regulatory approval to market its Tadalafil Tablets in 5mg, 10mg and 20mg strengths, developed by its wholly owned Zhuhai United Laboratories Zhongshan Branch. The product is a selective PDE5 inhibitor for erectile dysfunction, including cases associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia, aligning the company more deeply with the fast-growing men’s health segment in China.

Tadalafil’s prolonged duration of action of up to 36 hours and efficacy unaffected by food intake are expected to provide patients with greater flexibility and improve the drug’s clinical appeal relative to peers. The company says the new approval further enriches its product portfolio and should bolster its competitiveness and brand influence in men’s health, while ongoing investment in R&D aims to strengthen its innovation capacity and long-term value creation for shareholders.

The move underscores the group’s strategy of expanding into high-demand therapeutic areas where differentiated formulations can support both clinical outcomes and market positioning. Stakeholders may see the approval as a signal of continued pipeline productivity and regulatory execution, potentially improving the firm’s standing in China’s crowded pharmaceutical landscape.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3933) stock is a Buy
with a HK$20.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on The United Laboratories International Holdings stock,
see the HK:3933 Stock Forecast page.

More about The United Laboratories International Holdings

The United Laboratories International Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed pharmaceutical group incorporated in the Cayman Islands, focusing on the research, development and manufacturing of prescription drugs. Through subsidiaries such as Zhuhai United Laboratories, the company targets key therapeutic areas including men’s health, aiming to broaden its diversified product portfolio and strengthen competitiveness in the Chinese pharmaceutical market.

The group leverages its wholly owned production base in Zhuhai and Zhongshan to develop branded formulations, seeking to enhance its presence in specialized segments. By continually investing in innovation and expanding its range of approved medicines, the company positions itself to capture growing demand in China’s healthcare sector and deliver long-term value to shareholders.

Its strategy emphasizes combining clinical advantages with market differentiation, as seen in products designed for enhanced patient convenience and efficacy. This approach supports the company’s bid to reinforce its brand influence and secure a larger share of targeted therapeutic markets.

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co ( (HK:1349) ) has shared an update.

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co has subscribed to a principal-guaranteed structured deposit product from Bank of China, investing RMB150 million of temporarily idle proceeds raised from its public A-share issuance. The 90-day deposit, assessed by the bank as low-risk, offers a floating annualized return between 0.3500% and 1.6500%, linked to movements in the USD/CAD central parity rate.

Because one or more applicable percentage ratios under Hong Kong Listing Rules exceed 5% but remain below 25%, the arrangement is classified as a disclosable transaction requiring reporting and public announcement but not shareholder approval. The move underscores the company’s effort to enhance returns on surplus funds while maintaining capital safety, reflecting conservative treasury management amid ongoing biopharmaceutical operations.

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co has subscribed to a principal-guaranteed structured deposit product with SPD Bank, investing RMB210 million of idle self-owned funds generated from daily operations. CGN Power Co., Ltd. has announced an adjustment to the conversion price of its A Share convertible corporate bonds following the approval of its 2025 profit distribution plan. The RMB4.9 billion bonds, issued in July 2025 to non-specific investors and traded on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange, initially carried a conversion price of RMB3.67 per share.

data.description:

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Solis Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2227) ) has issued an update.

Solis Holdings Ltd., a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, manages a portfolio of financial investments to support its working capital and strategic opportunities. The group subscribes to and disposes of bonds and similar instruments as part of its capital management approach.

The company disclosed the sale on 30 June 2026 of a Seatrium Financial Services Pte. Ltd. bond on the open market for S$758,694, realising an unaudited gain of S$7,800 after a similar disposal in May. Combined, these transactions reach the threshold for a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules but remain exempt from shareholder approval, and Solis plans to use the proceeds for general working capital and potential new investments.

The disposal was executed at prevailing market prices, with the board considering the terms fair and reasonable and confirming that no connected persons of the company acquired the bonds. The issuer of the bond, a wholly owned subsidiary of Seatrium Limited in Singapore, provides financial and treasury services to its group, highlighting Solis’s ongoing exposure to regional credit markets while retaining flexibility to redeploy capital.

More about Solis Holdings Ltd.

Solis Holdings Ltd. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group engages in investment activities and capital management, holding financial instruments such as bonds for investment purposes and deploying proceeds to support general working capital needs and potential new investment opportunities.

The company operates within the broader financial and capital markets, using fixed-income securities and other investments to manage liquidity and generate returns. Its activities reflect a focus on portfolio adjustment and optimising the use of funds within regional markets including Singapore and Hong Kong.

data.description:

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group ( (HK:1093) ) has shared an update.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group has entered into a strategic collaboration, option and license agreement with AstraZeneca to develop novel siRNA-based small nucleic acid drug candidates for renal diseases across multiple indications, using CSPC’s proprietary siRNA discovery and extrahepatic targeted delivery platforms. Under the deal, the partners will jointly discover and develop pre-clinical candidates for two targets, with AstraZeneca holding options for global or ex‑China rights while CSPC retains China rights to one program, and CSPC stands to receive an upfront US$30 million plus up to US$1.74 billion in potential milestone payments and single‑digit royalties, underscoring its ambitions in cutting-edge RNA therapeutics and strengthening its position as an emerging innovation partner in the biopharmaceutical sector.

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has issued an update.

United Company Rusal has issued an overseas regulatory announcement noting that a Russian-language disclosure has been released on the Moscow Exchange and on the company’s website, in line with Hong Kong listing requirements. The notice underscores the company’s cross-market reporting obligations and provides updated information on its current board composition.

The company also reported that its chairman decided on July 1, 2026 to convene a board meeting scheduled for July 6, 2026. The sole agenda item is the approval of a transaction under article 23.1.21 of the company’s charter, signaling that the board will consider a potentially significant corporate action requiring formal authorization.
